@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+---
+title: OCSF mapping for all FortiGate log types
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+pr: 140
+created: 2026-03-21T17:28:46.407793Z
+---
+
+FortiGate logs can now be mapped to OCSF using `fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::map`:
+
+```tql
+from_file "fortigate.log" {
+  read_lines
+}
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::map line
+this = ocsf
+ocsf::derive
+ocsf::cast
+```
+
+All major FortiGate log types are covered:
+
+| Log type | OCSF class |
+|---|---|
+| `traffic/*` | Network Activity (4001) |
+| `utm/dns` | DNS Activity (4003) |
+| `utm/ips`, `utm/anomaly`, `utm/virus`, `utm/dlp` | Detection Finding (2004) |
+| `utm/ssh` | SSH Activity (4007) |
+| `utm/ssl` | Network Activity (4001) |
+| `utm/webfilter` | HTTP Activity (4002) |
+| `utm/emailfilter` | Email Activity (4009) |
+| `utm/app-ctrl` | Network Activity (4001) |
+| `utm/cifs` | SMB Activity (4006) |
+| `event/user`, `event/system` | Authentication (3002) |
+| `event/vpn`, `event/endpoint` | Tunnel Activity (4014) |
+| `event/wad` | Network Activity (4001) |
+| `event/wireless` | Detection Finding (2004) |
+| `event/connector`, `event/ha`, `event/fortiextender`, `event/router`, `event/security-rating` | Base Event |
+
+The mapping handles NAT (`trandisp`/`transip` → `proxy_endpoint`), interface zones (`srcintfrole`/`dstintfrole` → `endpoint.zone`), UTM risk scores (`crscore`/`crlevel` → `risk_score`/`risk_level_id`), and session timestamps (`duration` → `traffic.start_time`/`end_time`). Network context for Detection Finding events is placed in `evidences` rather than top-level endpoint fields.

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+---
+title: Unify Fortinet package around FortiGate operators
+type: breaking
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - codex
+created: 2026-03-17T08:59:41.370501Z
+---
+
+The new `fortinet` package is now the entry point for Fortinet integrations, with FortiGate support namespaced under `fortinet::fortigate::*`. Existing installs must uninstall `fortinet-fortigate` before installing the new `fortinet` package.
+
+Use the package to ingest framed FortiGate syslog and map it to OCSF:
+
+```tql
+subscribe "fortinet"
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::map
+publish "ocsf"
+```
+
+This replaces the previous `fortinet-fortigate` package layout and adds FortiGate parsing, cleanup, tests, and OCSF mappings for traffic, DNS, authentication, and selected security findings.

@@ -0,0 +1,64 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate anomaly/DoS logs → OCSF Detection Finding (2004)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 2
+ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create: a new anomaly detection
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.is_alert = true
+
+drop ocsf.unmapped.eventtype
+
+// `attack` is the anomaly/DoS signature name; `attackid` is its numeric ID.
+// Cache attackid as a temp field so both uid slots can reference it without
+// consuming it on first use (let doesn't support move).
+ocsf._attackid = (move ocsf.unmapped.attackid).string()
+ocsf.finding_info = {
+  uid: ocsf._attackid,
+  title: ocsf.unmapped.attack,
+  src_url: move ocsf.unmapped.ref,
+  analytic: {
+    type_id: 1,  // Rule
+    uid: ocsf._attackid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.attack,
+  },
+}
+drop ocsf._attackid
+
+// Network context goes into evidences for Detection Finding.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_endpoints
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::connection_info
+// Only emit evidences when at least one endpoint IP is present.
+if ocsf.src_endpoint.ip? != null or ocsf.dst_endpoint.ip? != null {
+  ocsf.evidences = [{
+    src_endpoint: move ocsf.src_endpoint,
+    dst_endpoint: move ocsf.dst_endpoint,
+    connection_info: move ocsf.connection_info,
+  }]
+} else {
+  drop ocsf.src_endpoint?
+  drop ocsf.dst_endpoint?
+  drop ocsf.connection_info?
+}
+
+// Security-control profile: `policytype` identifies the policy table (e.g.
+// "DoS-policy"); extend firewall_rule after the common UDO sets uid.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+if ocsf.unmapped.policytype? != null {
+  ocsf.firewall_rule.type = move ocsf.unmapped.policytype
+}
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    "clear_session": 6,   // Dropped
+    pass: 1,
+    block: 2,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::risk

@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate application control logs → OCSF Network Activity (4001)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.network_activity"
+
+// Application control events report traffic matched by app signatures.
+let $actions = {
+  pass: 6,    // Traffic
+  block: 5,   // Refuse
+  drop: 4,    // Fail
+  reset: 3,   // Reset
+}
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 4
+ocsf.class_uid = 4001
+ocsf.activity_id = $actions[ocsf.unmapped.action?]? else 0
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+drop ocsf.unmapped.eventtype
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_endpoints
+ocsf.dst_endpoint.hostname = move ocsf.unmapped.hostname
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::connection_info
+
+// `app` is the identified application; `appcat` is its category.
+if ocsf.unmapped.app? != null {
+  ocsf.app_name = move ocsf.unmapped.app
+}
+
+// `applist` is the application control profile; used as policy.name instead
+// of `profile` since app-ctrl logs use applist for the applied rule set.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+if ocsf.unmapped.applist? != null {
+  ocsf.policy = { name: move ocsf.unmapped.applist }
+}
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    pass: 1,
+    block: 2,
+    drop: 6,
+    reset: 21,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate user/system event logs → OCSF Authentication (3002)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.authentication"
+
+// FortiGate action → OCSF activity_id.
+let $actions = {
+  login: 1,
+  logout: 2,
+  authentication: 1,
+}
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3002
+ocsf.activity_id = $actions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// The authenticating user.
+ocsf.user = {
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.user,
+}
+
+// event/user: `group` is the local user group.
+if ocsf.unmapped.group? != null {
+  ocsf.user.groups = [{ name: move ocsf.unmapped.group }]
+}
+
+// event/system: `profile` is the admin privilege profile; presence implies
+// an administrative account.
+if ocsf.unmapped.profile? != null {
+  ocsf.user.type_id = 2  // Admin
+  ocsf.user.groups = [{ name: move ocsf.unmapped.profile }]
+}
+
+ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.srcip,
+  // event/user: `interface` is the ingress network interface.
+  interface_name: move ocsf.unmapped.interface?,
+}
+
+ocsf.dst_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.dstip,
+}
+
+// Both events involve a remote authentication attempt.
+ocsf.is_remote = true
+
+// `status` is the authentication outcome.
+if ocsf.unmapped.status? != null {
+  let $statuses = {
+    success: 1,
+    failed: 2,
+  }
+  ocsf.status_id = $statuses[move ocsf.unmapped.status]? else 0
+}
+
+// event/system: `method` is the access method (e.g. "ssh", "https").
+// event/user: `authproto` carries the protocol and source, e.g. "TELNET(ip)".
+if ocsf.unmapped.method? != null {
+  // auth_protocol_id = 99 (Other): SSH, HTTPS etc. are not in the OCSF enum.
+  // The string sibling is intentionally omitted; ocsf::derive rejects
+  // non-enum values for auth_protocol even when the id is Other.
+  ocsf.auth_protocol_id = 99
+  drop ocsf.unmapped.method?
+  ocsf.logon_type_id = 10  // Remote Interactive
+}
+if ocsf.unmapped.authproto? != null {
+  ocsf.auth_protocol_id = 99
+  drop ocsf.unmapped.authproto?
+  ocsf.logon_type_id = 8  // Network Cleartext
+  ocsf.is_cleartext = true
+}
+
+// event/system: `ui` duplicates method + srcip (e.g. "ssh(ip)"), drop it.
+drop ocsf.unmapped.ui?
+
+// Security-control profile: event/user carries policyid.
+if ocsf.unmapped.policyid? != null {
+  ocsf.metadata.profiles = ocsf.metadata.profiles.add("security_control")
+  ocsf.firewall_rule = {
+    uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.policyid).string(),
+  }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate CIFS/SMB inspection logs → OCSF SMB Activity (4006)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.smb_activity"
+
+// CIFS file filter: a file was blocked or allowed during a file transfer.
+// Map to File Open since the activity is a client trying to access a file.
+ocsf.category_uid = 4
+ocsf.class_uid = 4006
+ocsf.activity_id = 2  // File Open
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+drop ocsf.unmapped.eventtype
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_endpoints
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::connection_info
+
+// `filename` and `filesize` describe the file being transferred.
+// `filetype` stays in unmapped: type_name requires type_id sibling.
+ocsf.file = {
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.filename,
+  size: move ocsf.unmapped.filesize,
+}
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    blocked: 2,
+    allowed: 1,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate DLP logs → OCSF Detection Finding (2004)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 2
+ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.is_alert = true
+
+drop ocsf.unmapped.eventtype
+
+// `dlpextra` is the matched DLP rule/filter name; `filteridx` its index.
+ocsf.finding_info = {
+  uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.filteridx).string(),
+  title: move ocsf.unmapped.dlpextra,
+  analytic: {
+    type_id: 1,  // Rule
+    category: move ocsf.unmapped.filtertype,
+  },
+}
+
+// Network context goes into evidences for Detection Finding.
+// `hostname` is consumed by the network_evidence UDO.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_evidence
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    block: 2,
+    allow: 1,
+    log_only: 17,
+    quarantine: 3,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,55 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate DNS filter logs → OCSF DNS Activity (4003)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.dns_activity"
+
+// FortiGate eventtype → OCSF activity_id.
+let $activities = {
+  "dns-query": 1,
+  "dns-response": 2,
+}
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 4
+ocsf.class_uid = 4003
+ocsf.activity_id = $activities[move ocsf.unmapped.eventtype]? else 0
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_endpoints
+ocsf.src_endpoint.mac = move ocsf.unmapped.srcmac
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::connection_info
+
+ocsf.query = {
+  hostname: move ocsf.unmapped.qname,
+  class: move ocsf.unmapped.qclass,
+  packet_uid: move ocsf.unmapped.xid,
+  type: move ocsf.unmapped.qtype,
+}
+
+// DNS responses carry the resolved address(es).
+if ocsf.unmapped.ipaddr? != null {
+  ocsf.answers = [{
+    rdata: (move ocsf.unmapped.ipaddr).string(),
+    type: ocsf.query.type,
+    class: ocsf.query.class,
+  }]
+}
+
+// Security-control profile
+
+// Only response events carry an action (pass/block); queries are observation-only.
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    pass: 1,
+    block: 2,
+    drop: 6,
+    monitor: 17,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}
+
+// `profile` is the DNS filter profile (UTM security policy) applied to the session.
+// `policyid` is the firewall policy rule that matched the session, distinct from
+// the DNS filter profile above.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control

@@ -0,0 +1,49 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate email filter logs → OCSF Email Activity (4009)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.email_activity"
+
+// Email filter events are scan actions on in-transit email.
+ocsf.category_uid = 4
+ocsf.class_uid = 4009
+ocsf.activity_id = 3  // Scan
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+drop ocsf.unmapped.eventtype
+
+// `direction` maps to OCSF direction_id for the email flow (Email Activity
+// class carries this at the top level, not in connection_info).
+ocsf.direction_id = 0
+if ocsf.unmapped.direction? == "incoming" {
+  ocsf.direction_id = 1  // Inbound
+} else if ocsf.unmapped.direction? == "outgoing" {
+  ocsf.direction_id = 2  // Outbound
+}
+drop ocsf.unmapped.direction?
+
+// Email envelope fields.
+ocsf.email = {
+  from: move ocsf.unmapped.from,
+  to: [move ocsf.unmapped.to],
+  subject: move ocsf.unmapped.subject,
+  size: move ocsf.unmapped.size,
+}
+
+// `service` identifies the email protocol (IMAPS, SMTPS, POP3S).
+// Use move so network_endpoints does not also map it to dst_endpoint.svc_name.
+ocsf.protocol_name = move ocsf.unmapped.service
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_endpoints
+// Email Activity has no connection_info field; proto/sessionid stay in unmapped.
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    blocked: 2,
+    allowed: 1,
+    monitored: 17,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}

+---
+description: "FortiGate IPS logs → OCSF Detection Finding (2004)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+// IPS events are always new detections.
+ocsf.category_uid = 2
+ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// IPS detections are always alertable.
+ocsf.is_alert = true
+
+drop ocsf.unmapped.eventtype
+
+// `attack` is the signature name; `attackid` is its numeric ID;
+// `incidentserialno` is the unique identifier of this detection instance.
+ocsf.finding_info = {
+  uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.incidentserialno).string(),
+  title: ocsf.unmapped.attack,
+  src_url: move ocsf.unmapped.ref,
+  analytic: {
+    // IPS signatures are rule-based detections.
+    type_id: 1,
+    uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.attackid).string(),
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.attack,
+  },
+}
+
+// Network context goes into evidences for Detection Finding.
+// `hostname` is consumed by the network_evidence UDO.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_evidence
+
+// IPS is a security control: it detected and acted on the traffic.
+// `action` is the IPS response; map to disposition.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    dropped: 6,
+    pass: 1,
+    reset: 21,
+    block: 2,
+    alert: 19,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}
+
+// `crscore`/`crlevel` are the composite UTM risk score and level.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::risk

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate SSL inspection logs → OCSF Network Activity (4001)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.network_activity"
+
+// ssl-anomalies = connection blocked due to cert/TLS issue (Reset/Refuse).
+// ssl-exempt    = connection allowed to bypass SSL inspection (Traffic).
+let $activities = {
+  "ssl-anomalies": 3,  // Reset
+  "ssl-exempt": 6,     // Traffic
+}
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 4
+ocsf.class_uid = 4001
+ocsf.activity_id = $activities[move ocsf.unmapped.eventtype]? else 0
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_endpoints
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::connection_info
+
+// `reason` is the specific SSL inspection verdict (e.g. block-cert-invalid).
+if ocsf.unmapped.reason? != null {
+  ocsf.status_detail = move ocsf.unmapped.reason
+}
+
+// SSL inspection is a security control.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    blocked: 2,
+    exempt: 1,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,124 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate traffic logs → OCSF Network Activity (4001)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.network_activity"
+
+// FortiGate traffic action → OCSF activity_id.
+// Traffic logs are session-end summaries; action reflects how the session ended.
+let $actions = {
+  close: 2,         // normal TCP FIN
+  accept: 6,        // flow accepted (typical for UDP or sniffer)
+  "client-rst": 3,  // TCP RST from client
+  "server-rst": 3,  // TCP RST from server
+  deny: 5,          // blocked by policy before session established
+  drop: 4,          // packet dropped mid-session
+}
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 4
+ocsf.class_uid = 4001
+ocsf.activity_id = $actions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.srcip,
+  port: move ocsf.unmapped.srcport,
+  interface_name: move ocsf.unmapped.srcintf,
+  mac: move ocsf.unmapped.srcmac?,
+}
+
+ocsf.dst_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.dstip,
+  port: move ocsf.unmapped.dstport,
+  interface_name: move ocsf.unmapped.dstintf,
+  // `service` is the application-layer protocol name for the destination port.
+  svc_name: move ocsf.unmapped.service,
+}
+
+// `srcintfrole`/`dstintfrole` are the network zones of the ingress/egress
+// interfaces (e.g. "lan", "wan", "dmz"). Map to endpoint.zone and derive
+// connection direction from the pairing when possible.
+if ocsf.unmapped.srcintfrole? != null {
+  ocsf.src_endpoint.zone = ocsf.unmapped.srcintfrole
+}
+if ocsf.unmapped.dstintfrole? != null {
+  ocsf.dst_endpoint.zone = ocsf.unmapped.dstintfrole
+}
+ocsf.connection_info = {
+  protocol_num: move ocsf.unmapped.proto,
+  uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.sessionid).string(),
+  direction_id: 0,
+}
+if ocsf.unmapped.srcintfrole? == "lan" and ocsf.unmapped.dstintfrole? == "wan" {
+  ocsf.connection_info.direction_id = 2  // Outbound
+} else if ocsf.unmapped.srcintfrole? == "wan" and ocsf.unmapped.dstintfrole? == "lan" {
+  ocsf.connection_info.direction_id = 1  // Inbound
+}
+drop ocsf.unmapped.srcintfrole?
+drop ocsf.unmapped.dstintfrole?
+
+// `duration` is the session lifetime in seconds; `ocsf.time` is the end.
+ocsf.traffic = {
+  bytes_in: move ocsf.unmapped.rcvdbyte,
+  bytes_out: move ocsf.unmapped.sentbyte,
+  packets_in: move ocsf.unmapped.rcvdpkt?,
+  packets_out: move ocsf.unmapped.sentpkt,
+  end_time: ocsf.time,
+  start_time: ocsf.time - (move ocsf.unmapped.duration?).seconds(),
+}
+
+// `trandisp` is the NAT type applied; `transip`/`transport` are the translated
+// address/port. SNAT rewrites the source, DNAT rewrites the destination.
+// transip=0.0.0.0 means no effective translation (e.g. sniffer mode).
+if ocsf.unmapped.trandisp? == "snat" and ocsf.unmapped.transip? != 0.0.0.0 {
+  ocsf.src_endpoint.proxy_endpoint = {
+    ip: move ocsf.unmapped.transip,
+    port: move ocsf.unmapped.transport,
+  }
+} else if ocsf.unmapped.trandisp? == "dnat" and ocsf.unmapped.transip? != 0.0.0.0 {
+  ocsf.dst_endpoint.proxy_endpoint = {
+    ip: move ocsf.unmapped.transip,
+    port: move ocsf.unmapped.transport,
+  }
+}
+drop ocsf.unmapped.trandisp?
+drop ocsf.unmapped.transip?
+drop ocsf.unmapped.transport?
+
+// `app` is the application identified by deep packet inspection.
+if ocsf.unmapped.app? != null {
+  ocsf.app_name = move ocsf.unmapped.app
+}
+
+// `osname` is the source device OS as fingerprinted by FortiGate.
+if ocsf.unmapped.osname? != null {
+  ocsf.src_endpoint.os = {
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.osname,
+  }
+}
+
+// Traffic logs always reflect a firewall policy decision.
+ocsf.metadata.profiles = ocsf.metadata.profiles.add("security_control")
+
+// Firewall policy that matched the session.
+// `poluuid` is the policy UUID (absent in local/multicast/sniffer logs);
+// `policyid` is its numeric ID; `policytype` is the policy table
+// (e.g., "policy", "local-in-policy", "multicast-policy", "sniffer").
+ocsf.firewall_rule = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.poluuid?,
+  name: (move ocsf.unmapped.policyid).string(),
+  type: move ocsf.unmapped.policytype,
+}
+
+// `utmaction` is the outcome of the UTM/IPS engine, distinct from `action`
+// which reflects how the TCP/UDP session itself ended.
+if ocsf.unmapped.utmaction? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    allow: 1,
+    block: 2,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.utmaction]? else 0
+}
+
+// `crscore`/`crlevel` are the composite UTM risk score and level.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::risk

@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate antivirus logs → OCSF Detection Finding (2004)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 2
+ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.is_alert = true
+
+drop ocsf.unmapped.eventtype
+
+// `virus` is the malware name; `virusid` is the signature ID;
+// `dtype` is the detection type (e.g. "Virus", "Botnet").
+ocsf.malware = [{
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.virus,
+  uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.virusid).string(),
+  // `dtype` maps to classification: "Virus" → 19.
+  classification_ids: [19],
+  provider: "FortiGate",
+}]
+
+ocsf.finding_info = {
+  uid: ocsf.malware[0].uid,
+  title: ocsf.malware[0].name,
+  src_url: move ocsf.unmapped.ref,
+  analytic: {
+    type_id: 1,  // Rule
+    name: ocsf.malware[0].name,
+  },
+}
+
+// Network context goes into evidences for Detection Finding.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_evidence
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    blocked: 2,
+    allowed: 1,
+    monitored: 17,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}
+
+// `crscore`/`crlevel` are the composite UTM risk score and level.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::risk

@@ -0,0 +1,49 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate WAD SSL alert logs → OCSF Network Activity (4001)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.network_activity"
+
+// SSL fatal alerts terminate the connection.
+ocsf.category_uid = 4
+ocsf.class_uid = 4001
+ocsf.activity_id = 3  // Reset
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// `desc` is the human-readable reason for the SSL alert.
+if ocsf.unmapped.desc? != null {
+  ocsf.status_detail = move ocsf.unmapped.desc
+}
+
+// Only map endpoints when srcip is a real address.
+if ocsf.unmapped.srcip? != null and ocsf.unmapped.srcip != 0.0.0.0 {
+  ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+    ip: move ocsf.unmapped.srcip,
+    port: move ocsf.unmapped.srcport,
+  }
+} else {
+  drop ocsf.unmapped.srcip?
+  drop ocsf.unmapped.srcport?
+}
+
+ocsf.dst_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.dstip,
+  port: move ocsf.unmapped.dstport,
+}
+
+// `session_id` may be a hex string (e.g. "5f88ddd1") or integer 0.
+// Coerce to string before comparing to avoid type mismatch.
+if ocsf.unmapped.session_id? != null and (ocsf.unmapped.session_id).string() != "0" {
+  ocsf.connection_info = {
+    direction_id: 0,
+    uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.session_id).string(),
+  }
+} else {
+  drop ocsf.unmapped.session_id?
+}
+
+// SSL inspection is a security control.
+// `action` (send/receive) describes alert direction; consumed here.
+drop ocsf.unmapped.action?
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+ocsf.disposition_id = 2  // Blocked: fatal alert terminates the connection

@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate web filter logs → OCSF HTTP Activity (4002)"
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.http_activity"
+
+// Web filter logs report a URL access attempt; the HTTP method is unknown.
+ocsf.category_uid = 4
+ocsf.class_uid = 4002
+ocsf.activity_id = 0  // Unknown: FortiGate doesn't log the HTTP verb here
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+drop ocsf.unmapped.eventtype
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_endpoints
+// `hostname` is the virtual host name of the requested site.
+ocsf.dst_endpoint.hostname = move ocsf.unmapped.hostname
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::connection_info
+
+// `url` is the path; `hostname` (already mapped above) is the host.
+ocsf.http_request = {
+  url: {
+    hostname: ocsf.dst_endpoint.hostname,
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.url,
+  },
+}
+
+ocsf.traffic = {
+  bytes_in: move ocsf.unmapped.rcvdbyte,
+  bytes_out: move ocsf.unmapped.sentbyte,
+}
+
+// Web filter is a security control: it evaluated and acted on the request.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::security_control
+
+if ocsf.unmapped.action? != null {
+  let $dispositions = {
+    blocked: 2,
+    allowed: 1,
+    monitored: 17,
+  }
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.action]? else 0
+}
+
+// `crscore`/`crlevel` are the composite UTM risk score and level.
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::risk

@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+---
+description: "FortiGate wireless event logs → OCSF Detection Finding (2004) or Base Event"
+---
+
+// Rogue-AP events carry a `bssid` (the rogue AP's MAC address); routine
+// wireless telemetry (DHCP, handshakes, traffic stats) does not. Use bssid
+// presence as the structural discriminator rather than enumerating action
+// values, which can expand across firmware versions.
+if ocsf.unmapped.bssid? != null {
+  @name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+  ocsf.category_uid = 2
+  ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+  ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+  ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+  ocsf.is_alert = true
+
+  // `ssid` is the rogue AP name; `bssid` is its MAC (unique identifier).
+  ocsf.finding_info = {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.bssid,
+    title: f"Rogue AP: {move ocsf.unmapped.ssid}",
+    analytic: {
+      type_id: 1,  // Rule
+      name: move ocsf.unmapped.action,
+    },
+  }
+
+  // `snclosest` is the FortiAP serial number that detected the rogue AP.
+  if ocsf.unmapped.snclosest? != null {
+    ocsf.device = {
+      uid: move ocsf.unmapped.snclosest,
+      zone: ocsf.device.zone,
+    }
+  }
+} else {
+  fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::base
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+description: >-
+  Map FortiGate network fields into ocsf.evidences for Detection Finding.
+  Puts src_endpoint, dst_endpoint, and connection_info inside the evidence
+  artifact where Detection Finding can carry them.
+---
+
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::network_endpoints
+
+// `hostname` is consumed here before packing; parse_kv may parse
+// IP-shaped hostname values as ip_t so coerce to string.
+if ocsf.unmapped.hostname? != null {
+  ocsf.dst_endpoint.hostname = (move ocsf.unmapped.hostname).string()
+}
+
+// `direction` in Detection Finding logs (IPS, virus, DLP) describes the
+// inspected packet's orientation relative to the UTM engine, not the network
+// session direction. Dropping it here prevents it from inverting direction_id
+// for sessions whose actual direction can be inferred from intfroles.
+drop ocsf.unmapped.direction?
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::connection_info
+
+// Only emit evidences when at least one endpoint IP is present; an all-null
+// evidence record is unhelpful and drop_null_fields does not recurse into lists.
+if ocsf.src_endpoint.ip? != null or ocsf.dst_endpoint.ip? != null {
+  ocsf.evidences = [{
+    src_endpoint: move ocsf.src_endpoint,
+    dst_endpoint: move ocsf.dst_endpoint,
+    connection_info: move ocsf.connection_info,
+  }]
+} else {
+  drop ocsf.src_endpoint?
+  drop ocsf.dst_endpoint?
+  drop ocsf.connection_info?
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+---
+description: >-
+  Enrich an in-progress OCSF event with context from the RFC 5424 syslog
+  frame produced by read_syslog. Call this after
+  fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::map and before this = ocsf.
+
+  Populates metadata.loggers with the syslog sender: hostname →
+  logger.device.hostname, timestamp → logger.logged_time, app_name →
+  logger.name.
+---
+
+ocsf.metadata.loggers = [{
+  device: {
+    hostname: hostname,
+  },
+  logged_time: timestamp,
+  name: app_name,
+  log_format: "syslog",
+}]
+

@@ -0,0 +1,25 @@
+id: fortinet
+name: Fortinet
+author: Tenzir
+author_icon: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tenzir/library/main/.tenzir.svg
+package_icon: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tenzir/library/main/fortinet/package.svg
+
+description: |
+  [Fortinet](https://www.fortinet.com/) provides a broad portfolio of security
+  products for network, endpoint, cloud, and operational technology use cases.
+
+  This package is intended as the home for Fortinet integrations in the Tenzir
+  library. Its current focus is
+  [FortiGate](https://www.fortinet.com/products/next-generation-firewall),
+  parsing FortiGate key-value logs and providing OCSF mappings.
+
+categories:
+- mappings
+- sources
+
+inputs:
+  syslog-url:
+    name: Syslog URL
+    description: The endpoint (including URL scheme) where FortiGate sends syslog.
+    type: string
+    default: udp://0.0.0.0:514

@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+---
+name: Onboard FortiGate logs via Syslog UDP
+description: |
+  A pipeline for ingesting and parsing FortiGate logs received via UDP syslog.
+---
+
+from_udp "{{ inputs.syslog-url }}" {
+  read_syslog
+}
+publish "fortinet"

@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+---
+name: FortiGate → OCSF
+disabled: true
+---
+
+subscribe "fortinet"
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::map content
+fortinet::fortigate::ocsf::syslog
+this = ocsf
+ocsf::derive
+publish "ocsf"
